flows: user -> trip — type=association; label="creates"; explanation="a user creates a trip."
trip -> plan — type=composition; label="contains"; explanation="a trip contains one or more plans."
plan -> dailyplan — type=composition; label="contains"; explanation="a plan contains one or more dailyplans."
dailyplan -> weather — type=association; label="references"; explanation="a dailyplan references a weather."
trip -> category — type=association; label="includes"; explanation="a trip includes one or more categories."
placetovisit -> category — type=association; label="belongs to"; explanation="a placetovisit belongs to a category."
user -> creates trip -> trip is created -> trip contains plan -> plan contains dailyplan -> dailyplan references weather
this diagram models a travel planning system.
users create trips, which contain plans.
each plan contains daily plans, which reference weather conditions.
trips can be associated with categories.
placetovisit is associated with a category.
user -> main : registeruser();
main -> users database : verifyvaliddata();
main -> users database : saveuserdata();
main -> user : registeruser.“ok”;
main -> user : registeruser.“error”;
user -> main : getdata();
the diagram models the user registration process.
the process starts with the user calling `registeruser()`.
the main component validates the data and saves it to the database.
if the data is valid, the user receives a success message.
if the data is invalid, the user receives an error message.
sd user login -> user : userlogin(email, password);
user -> main login : userlogin(email, password);
main login -> users database : validuser(email, password);
users database -> main login : x = true;
main login -> sd user login : userlogin("ok");
users database -> main login : userlogin("error");
the diagram models a user login process.
the process starts with the user providing their email and password to the system.
the system validates the credentials against a database.
if the credentials are valid, the system proceeds with a "userlogin('ok')" message.
if the credentials are invalid, the system returns an "userlogin('error')" message.
sd update trip data -> main — type=start event; label="start"; explanation="initiates the process";
main -> user — type=message; label="updatetrip"; explanation="calls the updatetrip function";
main -> trips database — type=message; label="updatetripdata"; explanation="updates the trip data in the database";
main -> user — type=message; label="listtrip"; explanation="lists the trips";
start -> main -> user -> main -> trips database -> main -> user
the diagram models the process of updating trip data.
the user initiates the update process by calling the `updatetrip` function.
the `updatetrip` function updates the data in the trips database.
the user can then list the updated trips.
sd -> change user profile — type=initial node; label="change user profile"; explanation="start activity"
change user profile -> main — type=fork; label="getnewdata"
main -> users database — type=invoke; label="getnewdata"
users database -> main — type=return; label="x"
main -> main — type=fork; label="verifyvaliddata"
main -> users database — type=invoke; label="verifyvaliddata(x)"
users database -> main — type=return; label="y"
main -> main — type=fork; label="updateuserdata"
main -> users database — type=invoke; label="updateuserdata(y)"
users database -> main — type=return; label="ok"
main -> change user profile — type=join; label="ok"
main -> change user profile — type=fork; label="else"
main -> change user profile — type=invoke; label="error"
change user profile -> change user profile — type=return; label="error"
the activity models the process of changing a user profile.
the process starts with the "change user profile" activity.
it involves retrieving new data, validating the data, and updating the user data in the database.
there are two possible outcomes: success ("ok") or error.
the validation step checks if the data is valid, and if not, an error is returned.

pages.uml_type:
class
- User — kind=class; attributes=[username: String, password: String, name: String, email: String]; operations=[setUsername(username: String), setPassword(password: String), setName(name: String), setEmail(email: String), getUsername(), getPassword(), getName(), getEmail()]; role=User
- Trip — kind=class; attributes=[city: String, country: String, beginDate: Date, endDate: Date, author: User, id: int]; operations=[setCity(city: String), setCountry(country: String), setBeginDate(beginDate: Date), setEndDate(endDate: Date), setAuthor(author: User), setId(id: int)]; role=Trip
- Plan — kind=class; attributes=[id: int, trip: Trip, dailyPlan: DailyPlan]; operations=[setId(id: int), setTrip(trip: Trip), setDailyPlan(dailyPlan: DailyPlan)]; role=Plan
- DailyPlan — kind=class; attributes=[day: Date, date: Date, weather: Weather]; operations=[setDay(day: Date), setDate(date: Date), setWeather(weather: Weather)]; role=DailyPlan
- Category — kind=class; attributes=[name: String, id: int, icon: String]; operations=[setName(name: String), setId(id: int), setIcon(icon: String), getName(), getId(), getIcon()]; role=Category
- PlaceToVisit — kind=class; attributes=[name: String, id: int, category: Category, city: String, icon: String]; operations=[setName(name: String), setId(id: int), setCategory(category: Category), setCity(city: String), setIcon(icon: String), getName(), getId(), getCategory(), getCity(), getIcon()]; role=PlaceToVisit
- Weather — kind=class; attributes=[id: int, high: double, low: double, icon: String, description: String]; operations=[setId(id: int), setHigh(high: double), setLow(low: double), setIcon(icon: String), setDescription(description: String), getId(), getHigh(), getLow(), getIcon(), getDescription()]; role=Weather
